q: The world only exists in your eyes. You can make it as big or as small as you want.
h:
“The world only exists in your eyes. You can make it as big or as small as you want.” —
F. Scott Fitzgerald

q: Begin now to be what you will be hereafter.
h:
“Begin now to be what you will be hereafter.” —
St. Jerome

q: Instead of being intimidated by the limitations, be inspired to find new ways around them.
h:
“Instead of being intimidated by the limitations, be inspired to find new ways around them.” —
Ralph Marston

q: He will win who knows when to fight and when not to fight.
h:
“He will win who knows when to fight and when not to fight.” —
Sun Tzu

q: Life belongs to the living, and he who lives must be prepared for changes.
h:
“Life belongs to the living, and he who lives must be prepared for changes.” —
Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

q: Hold yourself responsible for a higher standard than anybody else expects of you.
h:
“Hold yourself responsible for a higher standard than anybody else expects of you.” —
Henry Ward Beecher

q: It's not the love you make. It's the love you give.
h:
“It's not the love you make. It's the love you give. ” —
Nikola Tesla

q: Make peace with your inner turmoil.
h:
“Make peace with your inner turmoil.” —
Unknown

q: Confidence is contagious. So is lack of confidence.
h:
“Confidence is contagious. So is lack of confidence.” —
Vince Lombardi

q: Be like a postage stamp. Stick to it until you get there.
h:
“Be like a postage stamp. Stick to it until you get there.” —
Bob Proctor

q: Nothing you do is going to matter that much in the long run. Don't take yourself so seriously.
h:
“Nothing you do is going to matter that much in the long run. Don't take yourself so seriously.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: The biggest room in the world is the room for improvement.
h:
“The biggest room in the world is the room for improvement.” —
Unknown

q: Life is much more successfully looked at from a single window.
h:
“Life is much more successfully looked at from a single window.” —
F. Scott Fitzgerald

q: Man suffers only because he takes seriously what the gods made for fun.
h:
“Man suffers only because he takes seriously what the gods made for fun.” —
Alan Watts

q: Every saint has a past, and every sinner has a future.
h:
“Every saint has a past, and every sinner has a future.” —
Oscar Wilde

q: No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now.
h:
“No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now.” —
Alan Watts

q: Everything you can imagine is real.
h:
“Everything you can imagine is real.” —
Pablo Picasso

q: True friendship ought never to conceal what it thinks.
h:
“True friendship ought never to conceal what it thinks. ” —
St. Jerome

q: Every traveler has a home of his own, and he learns to appreciate it the more from his wandering.
h:
“Every traveler has a home of his own, and he learns to appreciate it the more from his wandering.” —
Charles Dickens

q: Happiness cannot be traveled to, owned, earned, worn or consumed.
h:
“Happiness cannot be traveled to, owned, earned, worn or consumed.” —
Denis Waitley

q: All know the way; few actually walk it.
h:
“All know the way; few actually walk it. ” —
Bodhidharma

q: The grass is greener where you water it.
h:
“The grass is greener where you water it.” —
Neil Barringham

q: Realize deeply that the present moment is all you have. Make the NOW the primary focus of your life.
h:
“Realize deeply that the present moment is all you have. Make the NOW the primary focus of your life. ” —
Eckhart Tolle

q: Happiness is pretty simple: someone to love, something to do, something to look forward to.
h:
“Happiness is pretty simple: someone to love, something to do, something to look forward to.” —
Rita Mae Brown

q: There is no limit to the amount of good you can do if you don't care who gets the credit.
h:
“There is no limit to the amount of good you can do if you don't care who gets the credit.” —
Ronald Reagan

q: Whatever is begun in anger, ends in shame.
h:
“Whatever is begun in anger, ends in shame.” —
Benjamin Franklin

q: Resentment or grudges do no harm to the person against whom you hold these feelings but every day and every night of your life, they are eating at you.
h:
“Resentment or grudges do no harm to the person against whom you hold these feelings but every day and every night of your life, they are eating at you.” —
Norman Vincent Peale

q: Nature loves to hide.
h:
“Nature loves to hide.” —
Heraclitus

q: There's more to life than being a passenger.
h:
“There's more to life than being a passenger.” —
Amelia Earhart

q: Confidence is the greatest friend.
h:
“Confidence is the greatest friend.” —
Lao Tzu

q: In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.
h:
“In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.” —
Sun Tzu

q: Would you rather live your life according to the approval of others or aligned with your truth and your dreams?
h:
“Would you rather live your life according to the approval of others or aligned with your truth and your dreams?” —
Robin Sharma

q: Concentrated power has always been the enemy of liberty.
h:
“Concentrated power has always been the enemy of liberty.” —
Ronald Reagan

q: The size of your success is measured by the strength of your desire; the size of your dream; and how you handle disappointment along the way.
h:
“The size of your success is measured by the strength of your desire; the size of your dream; and how you handle disappointment along the way.” —
Robert Kiyosaki

q: Live and act within the limit of your knowledge and keep expanding it to the limit of your life.
h:
“Live and act within the limit of your knowledge and keep expanding it to the limit of your life.” —
Ayn Rand

q: Life should be touched, not strangled.
h:
“Life should be touched, not strangled. ” —
Ray Bradbury

q: If ever you feel like an animal among men, be a lion.
h:
“If ever you feel like an animal among men, be a lion.” —
Criss Jami

q: In order to carry a positive action, we must develop here a positive vision.
h:
“In order to carry a positive action, we must develop here a positive vision.” —
Dalai Lama

q: Inner peace is when you realize that, in the end, nothing matters.
h:
“Inner peace is when you realize that, in the end, nothing matters.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: It is not how much we have, but how much we enjoy, that makes happiness.
h:
“It is not how much we have, but how much we enjoy, that makes happiness.” —
Charles Spurgeon

q: Life is a long lesson in humility.
h:
“Life is a long lesson in humility. ” —
James Matthew Barrie

q: Your spirit is the true shield.
h:
“Your spirit is the true shield.” —
Morihei Ueshiba

q: The most important thing in communication is to hear what isn't being said.
h:
“The most important thing in communication is to hear what isn't being said.” —
Peter Drucker

q: Engage in those actions and thoughts that nurture the good qualities you want to have.
h:
“Engage in those actions and thoughts that nurture the good qualities you want to have.” —
Paramahansa Yogananda

q: The golden opportunity you are seeking is in yourself. It is not in your environment; it is not in luck or chance, or the help of others; it is in yourself alone.
h:
“The golden opportunity you are seeking is in yourself. It is not in your environment; it is not in luck or chance, or the help of others; it is in yourself alone. ” —
Orison Swett Marden

q: Great thoughts speak only to the thoughtful mind, but great actions speak to all mankind.
h:
“Great thoughts speak only to the thoughtful mind, but great actions speak to all mankind.” —
Theodore Roosevelt

q: When something is important enough, you do it even if the odds are not in your favor.
h:
“When something is important enough, you do it even if the odds are not in your favor.” —
Elon Musk

q: Change happens when the pain of holding on becomes greater than the fear of letting go.
h:
“Change happens when the pain of holding on becomes greater than the fear of letting go.” —
Spencer Johnson

q: Either get busy living or get busy dying.
h:
“Either get busy living or get busy dying.” —
Stephen King

q: You win more from losing than winning.
h:
“You win more from losing than winning.” —
Morgan Wootten
